facts.notes: NYC has NO municipal minimum-wage ordinance. The $17.00 downstate rate is set by STATE law for NYC / Long Island / Westchester. Recorded here because that is where the establishment-level lookup expects to find it, but the authority is the state, not the city.

NYC Earned Safe and Sick Time Act — 1 hr per 30 worked; 100+ employees accrue up to 56 hrs paid, 5-99 up to 40 hrs paid. A 2026-02-22 amendment layers 32 hrs UNPAID leave on top; the paid caps are unchanged.

NYC Fair Workweek: 14-day advance schedule; 'clopening' = shifts under 11 hrs apart, carrying a $100 premium; schedule-change premiums $10-$75. Covers fast food and retail. Authority is the official DCWP Fair Workweek Law page (covers both industries; the fast-food FAQ PDF at /assets/dca/downloads/pdf/workers/FAQs-FairWorkweek-FastFood.pdf is fast-food-only). nyc.gov returns 403 to automated fetch — the authority suite must read that as host-alive, not dead.
NYC Local Law 32 (as amended by LL 59), under the NYC Human Rights Law — 4+ employees must post a good-faith minimum and maximum salary in job, promotion, and transfer ads.
